content.problemStatement:
Professor GukiZ decided to distribute all of his candies to his NNN students (numbered 111 through NNN). Let's denote the number of candies GukiZ gave to the iii-th student by pip_ipi​. As GukiZ has a lot of students, he does not remember all the exact numbers of candies he gave to the students. He only remembers the following properties of the sequence ppp:


The numbers of candies given to each of the first KKK students (p1,p2,…,pKp_1, p_2, \dots, p_Kp1​,p2​,…,pK​) are known exactly.
All elements of the sequence ppp are distinct.
The difference between the maximum and minimum element of the sequence ppp does not exceed xxx.
The professor gave out the biggest possible total number of candies, i.e. S=p1+p2+p3+…+pNS = p_1 + p_2 + p_3 + \ldots + p_NS=p1​+p2​+p3​+…+pN​ is maximum possible.
Normally, professors never lie, so the information provided by GukiZ is valid and there is at least one sequence that satisfies all conditions. GukiZ would like to know the total number of candies SSS he had at the beginning. Can you help him find this number?
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N, K and x. The second line contains K space-separated integers p1, p2, …, pK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of candies GukiZ had.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Example case 1: There are four students. We know that the first student got p1 = 2 candies, the second student got p2 = 1 and the third got p3 = 5 candies; we don't know the number of candies given to the last student. The difference between the maximum and minimum number of candies given to students is already 5 - 1 = 4 = x, so the fourth student could only have been given between 1 and 5 candies. It can't be 5 either, so we maximize the number of candies if we give 4 candies to the fourth student. In total, there are 2 + 1 + 5 + 4 = 12 candies. Example case 2: We know the number of candies given to each of the two students, so we know that GukiZ had 3 + 6 = 9 candies.

solution.approach: To maximize the total number of candies, we need to maximize the value of each pi. We know the first K values of pi, and we need to find the remaining N-K values. Since all elements of the sequence p are distinct, we can start from the maximum value of the known pi and assign the next N-K values in decreasing order, making sure the difference between the maximum and minimum element does not exceed x.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * K) — we iterate over each test case and for each test case, we iterate over the known values of pi.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the input values and the result.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, K, x = map(int, input().split())
    p = list(map(int, input().split()))
    max_val = max(p)
    min_val = min(p)
    sum_val = sum(p)
    start = max_val + 1
    for _ in range(N - K):
        sum_val += start
        start += 1
        if start - min_val > x:
            break
    print(sum_val)
